objective: Add a reusable operator-facing subagent packet template/check path so manual delegated prompts consistently include the known tool-use guard, bounded scope, evidence, handoff fields, and closeout expectations instead of relying on memory.

packets.objective: Review whether the subagent packet template/check should be separate from task orchestration templates and identify tests/docs risks.
Expose subagent packet template/check commands through taskboard and ai facade without mutating task state.
Cover template contents, valid packets, weak guard rejection, unbounded allowed files, missing handoff fields, CLI aliases, and facade forwarding.
Document the reusable packet commands before manual spawn_agent launches.

budgets.context: Bounded to taskboard packet helpers, ai facade forwarding, subagent protocol docs, T0090 task/evidence/workflow files, and focused tests.
budgets.validation: Focused subagent-packet tests, full taskboard and ai facade tests, taskboard validation, orchestration preflight, workflow manifest check, review validation, and strict compact status evidence.

verification.strategy: machine gates plus independent design review and lead integration

integration.policy: Lead keeps task-level orchestration packets separate from prompt-level subagent packets, accepts both explicit and short aliases, and uses the packet check as friction reduction rather than mandatory task state.
